settings.research_heading: Research on Hearing Damage in Dentistry
settings.research_intro: Independent studies show that dental professionals face consistent high-frequency noise levels from scalers and handpieces that can lead to long-term hearing loss after just 5 years in practice.
settings.logos_heading: Trusted by dental professionals across Europe

settings.question: Can I wear Earasers during patient treatment?
settings.answer: Yes. The Smart Seal design keeps the earplug secure during normal head and jaw movement. Many hygienists wear them for their entire shift without removing them between patients.

settings.question: Will I still be able to hear my patients clearly?
settings.answer: Yes. The V-filter preserves the 250–300 Hz frequency range where human speech sits. You remain fully protected from drill and scaler noise while clearly hearing patient instructions and responses.

settings.question: Which filter is recommended for dental work?
settings.answer: We recommend the SNR 20 (Clinical Balance, −26 dB peak) for most dental environments. The SNR 14 (Gentle Standard) is a lighter option if you work in quieter practices.
